commit.message:
feat: i18n — locale system, map language switching, EN/FR translations (#1)

Add a full internationalisation layer following the Document First process.

## useLocale composable (src/core/useLocale.js)
- New `useLocale()` composable exposing `t(key)`, `locale`, `locales`,
  `localeNames`, `mapLanguageTag`, and `setLocale()`
- Language resolution order: user settings → Navigator.init locale option →
  browser navigator.language → English fallback
- Custom per-language message overrides via the `messages` init option

## Translations (src/locales/)
- Baseline EN and FR translation files (62 keys each) covering all UI strings
  in the about modal, side panel menu, locate feature, and settings panel

## Navigator.init() API additions (src/index.js)
- New `locale` option: sets the default language when no user preference is stored
- New `messages` option: per-language label overrides for white-labelling

## Map label language (src/core/useMap.js)
- `applyMapLanguage()` rewrites symbol-layer text-field to a coalesce
  expression `[name:XX, name, name:en]` for all OSM name-based layers
- Runs on map load and reactively whenever the active locale changes

## Settings language selector (src/features/settings/)
- `useSettings` gains `language` / `setLanguage` (stored in navigator_settings_*)
- Settings panel renders a language dropdown populated from `localeNames`

## UI components localised
- about.vue, menu.vue, top.vue, locate/button.vue, locate/panel.vue,
  settings/panel.vue — all static strings replaced with `t()` calls

## Docs
- New docs/locale.md: full locale API reference + OSM multilingual names guide

commit.message:
Fix SSAStyle initialization and ensure proper style names for Thai subtitles

Changes:
- Removed invalid 'name' parameter from SSAStyle constructor (not supported by pysubs2)
- Added explicit style_name handling in apply_style() and convert_srt_to_ass()
- Thai subtitles now correctly use 'Default-Thai' style name for all events
- Default (Noto Sans) subtitles use 'Default' style name for all events
- Style names are stored in SSAFile.styles dict, not on SSAStyle objects

This ensures that when SRT files are converted to ASS:
- All events get the correct style applied (Default or Default-Thai)
- Thai subtitles consistently use Arial font with Default-Thai style
- English/Indonesian subtitles consistently use Noto Sans with Default style
